title: Pavonia berteroi
intro: Pavonia berteroi, also known as the Red Pavonia or Red Rose Mallow, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can aid in treating wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pavonia berteroi include flavonoids, glycosides, and anthraquinones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal infusions, decoctions, and teas, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, making it a valuable resource in traditional and natural medicine.
taxonomy: Pavonia berteroi is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Pavonia, which consists of tropical and subtropical plants native to the Americas. The genus Pavonia is further classified under the order Malvales and the clade Rosidae. Within the family Malvaceae, Pavonia berteroi is part of a larger group of plants that include hibiscus, okra, and cotton. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Pavonia berteroi, also known as the Hawaiian rockrose or Hawaiian rosemallow, has been found to have potential medicinal properties.
